title: festuca rubra subsp. asperifolia
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. asperifolia, also known as red fescue, is a perennial grass species belonging to the Poaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. asperifolia include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
